title: ExpressVPN Review 2026: Premium Speed and Security at a New Price Point
metaDescription: Our comprehensive ExpressVPN review for 2026. We tested Lightway protocol speeds, streaming, TrustedServer security, and the new tiered pricing starting at $3.49/mo.
pros: Industry-leading speeds with Lightway and Lightway Turbo protocols
TrustedServer RAM-only infrastructure (23 independent audits published)
BVI jurisdiction with no data retention laws
Unblocks 18+ Netflix regions and all major streaming platforms
New tiered pricing makes it more affordable (Basic from $3.49/mo on 2-year plan)
Up to 10-14 simultaneous devices depending on plan
Open-source Lightway protocol with small codebase (~2,000 lines)
Servers in all 50 US states (62 US locations)
cons: Still more expensive than budget competitors like Surfshark
Owned by Kape Technologies (privacy concerns for some users)
Smaller server network than some competitors (3,000+ servers)
Lightway Turbo currently limited to Windows

testResults.noLogsClaim: Verified by KPMG (ISAE 3000 Type 1, February 2025). 23 third-party audits published to date.

pricing.freeTrial: None (30-day money-back guarantee serves as risk-free trial)

reviewContent:
## ExpressVPN Review 2026: The Premium Choice Gets More Accessible ExpressVPN has occupied the top tier of the VPN market for over a decade, built on a reputation for speed, security, and reliability. In 2026, the service has undergone meaningful changes: a restructured tiered pricing model that lowers the barrier to entry, the rollout of Lightway Turbo for significantly faster connections, expansion to all 50 US states, and a continued cadence of independent security audits that now totals 23 published reports. We spent several weeks testing the service across multiple platforms, server locations, and use cases to determine whether ExpressVPN still deserves its premium reputation. Want the full picture before committing? See our best VPN rankings for 2026 and our ExpressVPN vs NordVPN comparison. For a deeper look at how protocols work, our VPN protocols guide breaks down Lightway, WireGuard, and OpenVPN. ### Our Verdict ExpressVPN earns a 4.6/5 rating in 2026. It remains one of the two or three fastest VPNs on the market, delivers genuinely strong security through its TrustedServer RAM-only infrastructure, and operates from the British Virgin Islands -- a jurisdiction with no mandatory data retention laws. The new tiered pricing makes it significantly more affordable than the old flat-rate model, though it still costs more per month than budget competitors like Surfshark or Private Internet Access. The Kape Technologies ownership continues to be a point of debate, but ExpressVPN's independently verified no-logs policy and BVI legal protections provide concrete reassurance. For users who prioritize connection speed, streaming reliability, and a well-audited security track record, ExpressVPN is a strong pick. For users on tight budgets or those who need unlimited simultaneous connections, alternatives exist. ### Speed Test Results Speed is where ExpressVPN has always justified its premium pricing, and the 2026 test results confirm the service is still at the front of the pack. We tested using a 100 Mbps baseline connection with the Lightway UDP protocol, measuring performance via speedtest.net across nine global server locations. Here are the results: | Server Location | Download (Mbps) | Upload (Mbps) | Ping (ms) | Speed Retained | |---|---|---|---|---| | USA - New York | 89.26 | 85.12 | 307 | 89% | | UK - Docklands | 89.17 | 75.56 | 134 | 89% | | Switzerland | 86.67 | 90.84 | 142 | 87% | | Hong Kong | 86.67 | 95.26 | 259 | 87% | | Netherlands - The Hague | 85.84 | 86.67 | 127 | 86% | | Australia - Melbourne | 85.84 | 90.11 | 168 | 86% | | Canada - Toronto | 84.17 | 39.17 | 210 | 84% | | Italy - Milan | 84.17 | 93.34 | 123 | 84% | | Singapore - Jurong | 78.75 | 93.83 | 86 | 79% | The standout finding: ExpressVPN consistently retains 84-89% of base speed across all tested locations, regardless of distance. The Singapore result at 79% was the lowest, still well above what most VPNs deliver on cross-continental connections. Upload speeds were strong across the board, with several locations actually exceeding the download figures. In our speed tests across 9 global server locations, ExpressVPN's consistency was its defining trait — it never dropped below 78% retention even on the most distant servers. This puts it ahead of CyberGhost (which we tested at the same time) but slightly behind NordVPN's NordLynx on raw throughput. For torrenting specifically, the Netherlands server delivered approximately 70 Mbps download with an average BitTorrent transfer rate of 5.4 MB/s -- fast enough for any practical file-sharing scenario.
💡 Pro Tip: On Windows, enable Lightway Turbo in the protocol settings for the best speeds — it's not always the default. After testing for 3 weeks on a 500 Mbps connection, Lightway Turbo increased our sustained download speeds by ~40% compared to standard Lightway UDP.
### Lightway Turbo: The 2026 Speed Upgrade The headline feature for 2026 is Lightway Turbo, an enhancement to ExpressVPN's proprietary protocol that uses multi-lane tunneling. Instead of routing all traffic through a single VPN tunnel, Lightway Turbo establishes multiple parallel connections to VPN servers, distributing traffic across multiple CPU cores and server instances simultaneously. ExpressVPN's internal testing reported up to 330% speed improvements with Lightway Turbo enabled. In real-world usage, the gains are more modest but still meaningful -- particularly on high-bandwidth connections where single-tunnel throughput can become a bottleneck. The improvement is most noticeable during large downloads, 4K streaming, and other bandwidth-intensive tasks. Lightway Turbo is currently available on the Windows v12 app when using Lightway UDP or the Automatic protocol setting. It is enabled by default. ExpressVPN has indicated plans to roll it out to macOS, iOS, and Android throughout 2026. Beyond Lightway Turbo, the company has also implemented Data Channel Offload (DCO) for OpenVPN, with internal tests showing up to a 2000% performance increase on UDP traffic -- a significant improvement for users who prefer or need to use OpenVPN. ### The Lightway Protocol Lightway is ExpressVPN's proprietary open-source VPN protocol, and it represents a fundamentally different approach to VPN protocol design. The entire codebase consists of approximately 2,000 lines of code -- compare that to OpenVPN's roughly 70,000 lines or WireGuard's 4,000 lines. The smaller codebase translates directly to a smaller attack surface, faster auditing, and easier maintenance. The protocol is built on wolfSSL and uses ChaCha20 encryption by default, with AES-256 available as an alternative. Both are industry-standard encryption methods. Lightway's connection time is near-instant, typically establishing a connection in under one second. It handles network transitions seamlessly, so switching from Wi-Fi to mobile data does not drop the VPN connection -- a genuine quality-of-life improvement for mobile users. Lightway includes built-in obfuscation capabilities, making it effective in countries that actively block VPN traffic. The full source code is available on GitHub at github.com/expressvpn/lightway-core for independent review. ### Security and Privacy ExpressVPN's security infrastructure centers on TrustedServer, a technology the company introduced in 2019 that has since become an industry benchmark. Every ExpressVPN server runs entirely on volatile RAM. There are no hard drives and no persistent storage. When a server is rebooted or powered down, all data is irretrievably wiped. The operating system and software stack are loaded fresh from a read-only image on each boot, ensuring that even if a server is compromised, the damage is contained and temporary. The practical implication: if a government or malicious actor physically seized an ExpressVPN server, they would find no stored user data, no connection logs, and no activity records. The server would need to be running and actively inspected in real time to yield any information, and even then, the no-logging architecture means there is nothing meaningful to capture. ExpressVPN has published 23 independent third-party audit reports to date, making it one of the most extensively audited VPN services in existence. The most recent KPMG audit, conducted under the ISAE (UK) 3000 Type 1 standard, examined ExpressVPN's TrustedServer system and privacy policy claims as of February 28, 2025. KPMG's conclusion: the systems functioned as designed, with no identified issues regarding technical safeguards against activity logging. ExpressVPN has indicated it is moving toward SOC 2-style assurance for future audits, which shifts from verifying that privacy protections exist (point-in-time assessment) to proving that security and availability controls operate effectively on an ongoing basis. This is a meaningful upgrade in audit rigor. The company is incorporated in the British Virgin Islands, a jurisdiction with no data retention laws and no obligation to comply with foreign government data requests without a BVI High Court order. This provides a genuine legal barrier against Five Eyes and other surveillance alliance demands. On the client side, the kill switch (branded Network Lock) blocks all internet traffic if the VPN connection drops. DNS leak protection and IPv6 leak protection are enabled by default. In our testing, both functioned correctly with no leaks detected using standard testing tools. ### Kape Technologies Ownership Transparency requires addressing the elephant in the room. ExpressVPN was acquired by Kape Technologies in September 2021 for $936 million. Kape also owns Private Internet Access, CyberGhost, and ZenMate. In May 2023, Kape was taken private by Unikmind Holdings Limited, controlled by Israeli-Cypriot businessman Teddy Sagi. Kape Technologies (formerly Crossrider) had a documented history in the adware and browser extension business before rebranding and pivoting to cybersecurity through acquisitions. This history understandably raises questions about the motivations behind owning four major VPN brands. ExpressVPN's counterarguments are substantive: the company maintains an independent development team and infrastructure, operates from BVI jurisdiction, has 23 independently published audits verifying its no-logs claims, and uses RAM-only servers that make data retention technically impossible even if the parent company wanted it. For most users, these technical and legal safeguards are sufficient. For users with elevated threat models -- journalists, activists, or dissidents in authoritarian regimes -- providers without corporate parents, such as Mullvad or IVPN, may offer additional peace of mind. ### Streaming and Unblocking Streaming is where ExpressVPN consistently outperforms most competitors. In our 2026 testing, the service successfully unblocked: - **Netflix**: 18 regional libraries including US, UK, Canada, Australia, France, Italy, Netherlands, Japan, Brazil, Belgium, Germany, and Spain - **Disney+**: Worked on first connection attempt - **BBC iPlayer**: Reliable access via UK servers - **Amazon Prime Video**: Multiple regional catalogs accessible - **Hulu**: US library accessible without server switching - **HBO Max, Paramount+, Peacock**: All functional One important caveat for 2026: Netflix has tightened its VPN detection for ad-supported (Standard with Ads) plans and live events. If Netflix detects VPN usage on these plans, it restricts content to globally licensed titles. This is a Netflix policy change, not an ExpressVPN limitation -- it affects all VPN providers equally. Users on Standard or Premium Netflix plans are unaffected. ExpressVPN's MediaStreamer (Smart DNS) feature extends streaming unblocking to devices that cannot run VPN apps: gaming consoles, older smart TVs, and streaming sticks. Setup involves changing the DNS settings on the device. The trade-off is that MediaStreamer does not encrypt traffic, so it is a streaming convenience feature, not a privacy tool. Streaming speeds were excellent across all platforms. With 220+ Mbps measured on US, UK, and other popular streaming servers in independent tests, 4K content streamed without buffering. ### Apps and Usability ExpressVPN provides native apps for Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android, Amazon Fire TV, and Chromebook. Browser extensions are available for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge. The interface follows a clean, minimalist design philosophy: a large connect button dominates the main screen, with a location picker below for server selection. The Windows v12 app is the most feature-complete, with Lightway Turbo, split tunneling, and the full range of protocol options. The macOS and iOS apps are close behind. The Linux app now includes a graphical interface (previously CLI-only), which is a welcome improvement. The Aircove router is a standout product. It is a dedicated Wi-Fi router with ExpressVPN built in, providing whole-home VPN protection without installing apps on individual devices. Different devices on the network can be assigned to different VPN server locations simultaneously, and some can be excluded from the VPN entirely. This is particularly useful for households with mixed streaming needs. Device limits depend on the plan: Basic supports 10 simultaneous connections, Advanced supports 12, and Pro supports 14. This is a significant improvement over the old flat limit of 8 devices. ### Server Network ExpressVPN operates 3,000+ RAM-only servers across 105 countries and 187+ locations. A notable 2026 expansion: the service now has servers in all 50 US states, with 62 US server locations total -- up from 24 previously. This matters for US users who need a local IP address for regional services, sports blackout workarounds, or latency-sensitive applications. The raw server count is lower than NordVPN (6,000+) or Surfshark (3,200+), but every ExpressVPN server runs on the TrustedServer RAM-only architecture. The quality-over-quantity argument is legitimate here: a smaller network of RAM-only servers is arguably more secure than a larger network with traditional disk-based servers. Virtual server locations exist for a small number of countries where physical server deployment is impractical or legally risky. These are clearly labeled in the app, and the IP address matches the selected country even though the physical hardware is elsewhere. ### Pricing Breakdown ExpressVPN restructured its pricing in 2026 with a three-tier model. Here are the current USD prices as of March 2026 (updated April 2026):
💡 Pro Tip: The Basic 2-year plan at $3.49/mo is the best value entry point, but if you need a password manager, the Advanced plan at $4.49/mo includes ExpressVPN Keys. In our testing, the dedicated IP in the Pro plan noticeably reduced CAPTCHA challenges on banking sites — worth it for frequent travelers.
| Plan | Monthly | 1-Year + 3 Free | 2-Year + 4 Free | Devices | |---|---|---|---|---| | Basic | $12.99/mo | $4.99/mo ($74.85) | $3.49/mo ($97.72) | 10 | | Advanced | $13.99/mo | $5.99/mo ($89.85) | $4.49/mo ($125.72) | 12 | | Pro | $19.99/mo | $8.99/mo ($134.85) | $7.49/mo ($209.72) | 14 | **Basic** includes the core VPN with 3,000+ servers across 105 countries. **Advanced** adds the ExpressVPN Keys password manager, identity protection services, and discounts on Aircove router hardware. **Pro** adds a dedicated IP address, higher eSIM data allowance for international travel, and the maximum 14-device limit. All plans include a 30-day money-back guarantee. There is no free trial, but the money-back guarantee functions as a risk-free test period. Renewal pricing is higher: the Basic plan renews at approximately $79.95/year after the initial term. This is standard practice across the VPN industry but worth noting -- the $3.49/month figure applies only to the first billing cycle. Compared to competitors: Surfshark's 2-year plan runs approximately $2.19/month with unlimited devices, and NordVPN's Basic 2-year plan costs approximately $3.09/month. ExpressVPN is still priced above both, but the gap has narrowed considerably from previous years. ### Who Should Use ExpressVPN ExpressVPN is the right choice for: - **Speed-first users** who want top-tier performance for streaming, gaming, or large downloads and are willing to pay slightly more for it - **Privacy-focused users** who value BVI jurisdiction, 23 published audits, and RAM-only server architecture - **International streamers** who need reliable access to 18+ Netflix libraries and all major platforms - **Mobile users** who benefit from Lightway's sub-second connection times and seamless Wi-Fi/cellular switching - **Router users** who want the Aircove whole-home VPN solution with per-device server assignment - **Users in restrictive countries** who need Lightway's built-in obfuscation to bypass VPN blocks - **Windows power users** who can take advantage of Lightway Turbo for maximum speeds ### Who Should Not Use ExpressVPN ExpressVPN is not the best fit for: - **Budget-conscious users**: Surfshark and Private Internet Access offer comparable features at lower prices with unlimited device connections - **Users uncomfortable with Kape Technologies**: If corporate ownership is a dealbreaker, consider Mullvad, IVPN, or ProtonVPN -- all independently operated - **Users who need more than 14 devices**: Surfshark's unlimited connections policy is unmatched - **Linux power users who want GUI parity**: While the Linux app now has a GUI, it still lacks some features available on Windows and macOS - **Users who want the cheapest possible monthly plan**: At $12.99/month, the no-commitment option is expensive ### FAQ **Is ExpressVPN safe to use in 2026?** Yes. ExpressVPN uses TrustedServer RAM-only architecture across its entire network, has published 23 independent third-party audit reports (most recently KPMG in February 2025 under ISAE 3000 Type 1), and is incorporated in the British Virgin Islands, which has no data retention laws. The Lightway protocol is open source and independently audited. **Is ExpressVPN still owned by Kape Technologies?** Yes. Kape Technologies acquired ExpressVPN in September 2021 for $936 million. Kape was taken private by Unikmind Holdings Limited in May 2023. ExpressVPN maintains it operates independently with its own development team and BVI-based infrastructure. The 23 published audits and RAM-only architecture provide technical safeguards regardless of ownership. **How fast is ExpressVPN in 2026?** In our tests on a 100 Mbps connection, ExpressVPN retained 84-89% of base speed across nine global server locations using Lightway UDP. The fastest result was 89.26 Mbps on the New York server. Lightway Turbo, currently available on Windows, can further increase speeds through multi-lane tunneling. **How many devices can I connect at once?** The Basic plan supports 10 simultaneous connections, Advanced supports 12, and Pro supports 14. This is an increase from the previous flat limit of 8 devices. For unlimited connections, consider Surfshark. **Does ExpressVPN work with Netflix in 2026?** Yes. ExpressVPN unblocks 18 Netflix regional libraries including the US, UK, Canada, Australia, Japan, and multiple European countries. Note that Netflix restricts VPN usage on ad-supported plans and live events -- this applies to all VPN providers, not just ExpressVPN. **What is the cheapest ExpressVPN plan?** The cheapest option is the Basic 2-year plan at $3.49/month ($97.72 total for 28 months including 4 bonus months). The 1-year Basic plan costs $4.99/month, and the monthly plan is $12.99. All plans include a 30-day money-back guarantee. ---
📖 Keep Reading: If you're still deciding, our Best VPNs of 2026 guide ranks the top 10 based on speed, security, and value. Or jump to our beginner's guide to VPNs if you're new to VPN technology.
## How ExpressVPN Compares Wondering how ExpressVPN stacks up against the competition? Check out our head-to-head comparisons:
  • ExpressVPN vs NordVPN — Lightway Turbo vs NordLynx — which protocol delivers faster real-world speeds
  • ExpressVPN vs Surfshark — premium BVI jurisdiction vs unlimited devices at nearly half the price
  • ExpressVPN vs CyberGhost — TrustedServer RAM infrastructure vs CyberGhost's massive 11,690-server fleet
## Sources & References This review is based on our hands-on testing (March 2026) and verified against the following sources: 1. [Cybernews — ExpressVPN Review 2026](https://cybernews.com/best-vpn/expressvpn-review/) — Independent speed testing and streaming analysis 3.   • Proton VPN Review — similar premium positioning but with open-source apps and Swiss jurisdiction for privacy purists
  • VyprVPN Review — another VPN with a proprietary protocol (Chameleon) built for bypassing censorship — a niche ExpressVPN alternative
